zh.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris是Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris的兒子，於大約1258年出生在威爾士。他至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris是Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris的儿子，于大约1258年出生在威尔士。他至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: ab
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  African/Muslim Amadou, Ousmane, Demba, Mamadou, Daouda, Ibrahima, Mohamed, Thierno, Abdoul, Abdoulaye, Abou, Aissatou, Fatime, Fatou, Mame, Moussa, Oumar, Yassin.  Vietnamese Dung, Thai Van, Tho, Thuy, Toan, Tong Van, Tran.
West African (mainly Senegal and Mauritania): Fulani name, from the Mandinka adjective ba(a) ‘big, great’. Compare Bah 1, see also Balde 1.
Muslim: abstracted as a surname from bā, a dialect form of Arabic Abu ‘father’, used in compound surnames such as Bā Wazīr and Bā Ṭarafi.

variants.name: Balde
meanings: West African (mainly Senegal and Mauritania): Fulani name, regarded as an equivalent (variant) of   Ba   1.
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with bald ‘bold, quick’ (see Bald ).
Catalan (Baldé): possibly from an old personal name of ancient Germanic origin (see 2 above).

variants.name: Bah
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  African/Muslim Mamadou, Thierno, Amadou, Boubacar, Fatmata, Fatoumata, Mariama, Oumou, Umaru, Aboubacar, Aissatou, Alieu, Mohamed, Abdulai, Ibrahima, Abdul, Abdoul, Ibrahim, Abou, Abu, Abubakar, Ali, Hassane, Mahmoud.
West African (mainly Guinea, also common in the Gambia and Sierra Leone): variant of the Fulani name   Ba   1.
Slovenian: probably a nickname from a derivative of bahati se ‘to boast, to pride oneself’. Compare Bach 7.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .
English: variant of   Pape   1.

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Madog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French Canadian: variant of   Madore  .

variants.name: Madore
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Eusebe, Fernand, Lucien, Normand, Adhemar, Adrien, Donat, Elzear, Francois, Gaetan, Gilles.
French Canadian: from a French shortened form of the Spanish personal name Amador, from Latin Amator (from amator ‘lover’), a Christian saint's name. Compare Mador .
In some cases possibly also Breton (Madoré): nickname meaning literally ‘good help’, from Old Breton mat ‘good’ and uuoret ‘help’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Leuaf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the vocabulary word for the part of a plant (Old English lēaf). This was one of the names taken from the world of nature in the 1960s under ‘hippy’ influence, and it has not been enduringly popular. Choice as a given name may have been influenced by the Scandinavian name Leif, from Old Norse Leifr, meaning ‘heir’.

en.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris was born about 1258, in Wales as the son of Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris. He had at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris est né environ 1258, à Galles, fils de Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris. Il a eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris wurde um 1258 in Wales, als Sohn von Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris è nato il circa 1258 nel Galles come figlio di Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudrisは約1258年にウェールズでMareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudrisの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris(은)는 대략 1258년 웨일스에서 Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris nasceu aproximadamente 1258, em Gales filho de Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris родился около 1258 г. в местности Уэльс. Его отца звали Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris nació aproximadamente 1258, en Gales como hijo de Mareddydd Ap Madog of Bodiudris. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija.
